items.title: Dunlop revs up Malaysia expansion plans
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dunlop is expanding its presence in Malaysia by growing its dealer network, widening its tyre portfolio and strengthening partnerships with automotive service providers. The global tyre brand, managed by Toyotsu Binter Malaysia Sdn Bhd and backed by Japan's Sumitomo Rubber Industries, sees Malaysia as a key growth market despite production being shifted overseas. Toyotsu Binter Malaysia managing director Marcus Lim said Dunlop has expanded its nationwide dealer network to about 250 outlets from around 150 since May.

items.title: DBKL anti-begging sweep nets 25 across Kuala Lumpur tourist belt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) said it took enforcement action against 25 people during a joint anti-begging operation across several city centre hotspots, with five later handed over to other government agencies for further action. DBKL said those subjected to enforcement action comprised 13 Malaysians and 12 foreign nationals. DBKL said it would continue strengthening strategic cooperation with enforcement and government agencies to curb begging activities in the capital.

items.title: Police probe viral bus stop stabbing near Wangsa Maju university
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating a viral video showing a man being attacked with a sharp weapon while waiting at a bus stop near a university in Wangsa Maju. Wangsa Maju district police chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman confirmed that police received a report from the victim. The viral video shows a man dressed in black holding a sharp weapon suddenly attacking a man in white, repeatedly kicking and stabbing him.

items.title: PSSC sets two-week deadline for govt stance on Lynas US defence ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has been given a two-week deadline to issue its official position on Lynas Rare Earths' supply agreements with the United States defence industry, amid concerns over the deal's implications for Malaysia's interests and foreign policy stance. In a statement today, the committee recommended that the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, Foreign Ministry, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry, and Science, Technology and Innovation Ministry seek legal advice from the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) on the agreements to safeguard Malaysia's interests. Chaired by Subang member of Parliament Wong Chen, the committee said the recommendations followed a meeting to examine Lynas' rare earth operations and supply agreements with the Pentagon, including their potential implications for Malaysia.

items.title: Sabah residents told not to panic over low-flying military aircraft, says RMAF
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The public in parts of Sabah's east coast have been advised not to panic if they observe low-flying aircraft or the presence of defence and security assets as the Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) conducts an exercise beginning Monday. The RMAF said Exercise Kelawar, which will be conducted until July 29, would involve tactical low-altitude flights and maritime asset movements in designated training areas covering Tawau, Semporna and Lahad Datu under the Op Pasir Selatan area. The exercise will also involve cooperation from special forces elements from the three services, namely the RMAF Special Forces, Royal Malaysian Navy Special Forces and the Army's Special Service Group.

items.title: Six charged with possessing more than six tonnes of subsidised cooking oil without licence
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 17 — Six men, including a Malaysian, were charged in the Sessions Court here today with possessing a total of 6,443 kilogrammes of subsidised cooking oil without a valid licence last Tuesday. Lorry driver Moo Teck Lim, 50, pleaded not guilty after the charge was read to him in Mandarin before Judge Fatimah Zahari. They were charged under Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974, an offence punishable under Section 22(1) of the Control of Supplies Act 1961, read together with Section 21 of the same Act and Section 34 of the Penal Code.

items.title: Social media influencer charged with raping underage girl
items.summary: KANGAR: A 23-year-old social media influencer was charged at the Sessions Court today with raping an underage girl last month. Muhammad Iman Hazriq Azizan pleaded not guilty after the charge was read out before Judge Sharifah Norazlita Syed Salim Idid. Kangar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop previously said the arrest followed a report lodged by the victim.

items.title: Malaysia's June inflation eases to 1.9pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation eased to 1.9 per cent in June from 2.0 per cent in May, the Statistics Department said. The moderation was mainly due to the transport group, which rose 2.8 per cent in June compared with 3.8 per cent in May. Labuan posted 2.3 per cent, Kuala Lumpur 2.2 per cent, while Kedah and Putrajaya both recorded 2.0 per cent.

items.title: Police warn against spreading false or provocative social media posts during Negeri Sembilan polls
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 17 — Police have reminded the public to use social media responsibly throughout the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, including by refraining from spreading defamatory, seditious, insulting, provocative or false content. Negeri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) viewed seriously any dissemination of content that could undermine public order, threaten national security or disrupt social harmony. Alzafny said police would intensify monitoring of social media content throughout the election period, and any breaches of the law would be investigated regardless of the background or status of those involved.

items.title: PM Anwar: Biggest barrier to reform is resistance to change
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the biggest challenge in implementing national reforms stems not from a lack of technology or expertise, but from the reluctance of certain quarters to embrace change, including among members of the elite who continue to defend a culture of corruption and systemic weaknesses. Anwar said the government’s efforts to strengthen governance and combat corruption might not be popular, but they must continue to ensure the country’s administrative system becomes more transparent, accountable and effective. Anwar said that, according to the theory of change, society is generally divided into two groups: those with a propensity to change and those who exhibit resistance to change.

items.title: Govt won't interfere in MMC Port's management appointments, says Loke
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government does not interfere in the management decisions of port operators, including the appointment of foreign executives, as long as ownership requirements involving strategic assets are maintained. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the ministry only regulates matters related to shareholding, particularly for strategic assets such as ports, which must remain majority-owned by Malaysian interests. "As far as the government is concerned, we regulate shareholding.

items.title: Two men remanded over murder of 10-year-old found dead in Sepanggar
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — Two men — a foreign national and a local — were remanded for seven days, from today, to assist in the investigation into the murder of a 10-year-old girl whose body was found in Kampung Numbak, Sepanggar, yesterday. The remand order for the two suspects, aged 39 and 42, was issued by Magistrate Wan Farrah Farriza Wan Ghazali until July 24. Earlier, the two suspects were arrested following the discovery of the body of Nurjilya Abdullah, who had been reported missing since July 13.

items.title: PN-BN understanding boosts Pas hopes in Negri Sembilan polls
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas is confident of winning all five state seats it is contesting in the Negri Sembilan election, following an understanding reached between Perikatan Nasional and Barisan Nasional. Its vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said the arrangement has strengthened the party's chances, including in the two seats it is seeking to wrest, Klawang and Ampangan. The five seats are among 11 contested by PN in the state election.

items.title: Taiwan tech rout drags Asian equities lower
items.summary: The MSCI EM Asia equities index slumped as much as 2.3 per cent, with Taiwanese stocks, which make up nearly 33 per cent of the index, dropping nearly six per cent to a five-week low. The MSCI gauge has lost nearly five per cent in two sessions so far. The semiconductor-heavy Taiwanese benchmark declined for a second day, with TSMC, the world's top contract chipmaker and a key Nvidia and Apple supplier, losing more than five per cent to its lowest in nearly three weeks, despite record quarterly profit.

items.title: Social media influencer pleads not guilty to rape of 14-year-old girl in Kangar
items.summary: KANGAR, July 17 — A social media influencer pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to raping a girl aged 14 years and 10 months, at a house in Jalan Tiong, Taman Kangar on June 17. Muhammad Iman Hazriq Azizan, 23, made the plea after the charge against him was read out before Judge Sharifah Norazlita Syed Salim Idid. The court fixed August 14 for mention of the case, with Deputy Public Prosecutor Nabilah Ahmad Poad appearing for the prosecution, while the accused was unrepresented.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia higher at midday on stronger-than-expected Q2 GDP
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Malayan Banking Bhd gained 18 sen to RM11.22, Public Bank Bhd added three sen to RM5.23, CIMB Group Holdings Bhd was nine sen firmer at RM7.79, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd was flat at RM14.30, but IHH Healthcare Bhd lost 13 sen to RM8.40. As for the active stocks, Tanco Holdings Bhd rose two sen to 34 sen, Ekovest Bhd added one sen to 21 sen, NationGate Holdings Bhd slid seven sen to RM1.10, Dagang NeXchange Bhd eased two sen to 44.5 sen and VS Industry Bhd was half a sen weaker at 23 sen. Of the top gainers, Hong Leong Bank Bhd increased 32 sen to RM22.34, Padini Holdings Bhd added 16 sen to RM1.58, Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd rose 14 sen to RM18.82, while Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd gained 13 sen to RM4.71. Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the losers list, falling RM1 to RM44.28, while Nestle (Malaysia) Bhd decreased 84 sen to RM92.16, United Plantations Bhd shed 48 sen to RM34.52 and UMS Integration Ltd was down 32 sen to RM7.71.

items.title: Indonesian woman sentenced to five years for unlawful abortion in Sarawak
items.summary: KUCHING, July 17 — The Sessions Court here has sentenced an Indonesian woman to five years’ imprisonment for intentionally taking misoprostol to medically abort her unborn son. Judge Musli Ab Hamid convicted Nopika, 21, on her guilty plea under Section 315 of the Penal Code for an act done with intent to prevent a child being born alive or to cause it to die after birth. According to the facts of the case, a medical officer attended to Nopika on the night of the incident after she complained of abdominal pain.

items.title: Man cleared of murder abetment fined RM2,500 for extorting restaurant owner
items.summary: KLANG: A man acquitted of an abetment to murder charge three months ago was fined RM2,500 after admitting to extorting a female restaurant owner. Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat fined Mok Kum Loon, 41, after he pleaded guilty to intentionally putting the 24-year-old victim in fear of injury to extort her. The offence was committed less than a month after Mok was acquitted by the High Court of conspiring to murder the husband of a freelance female interpreter on April 24.

items.title: Anwar says AI-ready teachers must not lose sight of ethics
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Malaysia’s education system must produce teachers who not only master emerging fields in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM), such as artificial intelligence (AI), but are also grounded in ethics and human values, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Also present were Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and IPGKPT director Kartini Abdul Mutalib. What must be upheld are human values.

items.title: School reputations cannot come before bullied children, says Anwar
items.summary: NILAI, July 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded schools not to conceal bullying incidents to protect their reputation, stressing that swift action is essential to safeguard victims and address the problem effectively. Anwar said this at the Temu Anwar programme with students and staff of the Institute of Teacher Education (IPG) Technical Education Campus in Bandar Enstek today. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek was also present.

items.title: Langkawi police probe childcare centre after former teacher alleges children locked in toilet
items.summary: LANGKAWI, July 17 — Police are investigating a report lodged by a former teacher at a childcare centre in the district alleging the improper treatment of children at the premises. Langkawi police chief ACP Khairul Azhar Nuruddin said police had received the report on Wednesday from a woman who had been dismissed from the centre. Khairul Azhar said police were continuing their investigation to establish the validity of the allegations before taking any further action.

items.title: MOHE data show 96.6pc of public university undergraduates are Malaysians, foreigners mostly postgraduates
items.summary: According to the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE), data from the MyMoheS system as of December 31, 2025, showed that international students accounted for 8.9 per cent of total enrolment at public universities, comprising 56,565 of the 634,706 students. MOHE said the majority of international students were enrolled in postgraduate programmes, while undergraduate programmes at public universities continued to give priority to local students. Overall, enrolment at public and private higher education institutions stood at 1,264,541 students, with international students making up 12.6 per cent, or 159,138 students.

items.title: KL’s Jalan Raja to close from July 21 to 27 ahead of Citrawarna 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Jalan Raja will close for nearly six days from next week as Kuala Lumpur prepares for Citrawarna 2026, with additional traffic restrictions planned during the festival’s cultural parade, Tourism Malaysia said today. Jalan Raja in front of Dataran Merdeka will be fully closed from noon on July 21 until 5am on July 27 to facilitate preparations, event operations and dismantling works for the annual festival. The closures will affect the route between Bulatan Dato’ Onn and Jalan Parlimen, extending to Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Esfahan, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and Jalan Tun Perak, as well as the Jalan Kinabalu access road to Jalan Sultan Hishamuddin.

items.title: Malaysia’s economy expands 5.8pc in Q2, manufacturing and mining drive growth, says DOSM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Malaysia’s economy expanded by 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026), following the 5.4 per cent growth in the preceding quarter, according to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM). “Overall, Malaysia’s economy grew at 5.6 per cent for the first half of the year (H1 2026), compared to 4.5 per cent in H1 2025,” it said in a statement today. “The construction sector grew moderately at 6.6 per cent (1Q 2026: 7.7 per cent), supported by the non-residential buildings sub-sector, particularly data centre construction projects and specialised construction activities,” it said.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia needs teachers skilled in new technologies, rooted in human values
items.summary: NILAI: Malaysia's education system must produce teachers who not only master emerging fields in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM), such as artificial intelligence (AI), but are also grounded in ethics and human values. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that in the face of rapid global change, education cannot simply continue with existing curricula and teaching methods but must remain dynamic to ensure the country does not fall behind in adopting new technologies. What must be upheld are human values.

items.title: PN-BN electoral understanding aims to wrest Negri Sembilan from PH
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The 'understanding' between Perikatan Nasional and Barisan Nasional in the upcoming Negri Sembilan state election is aimed at wresting control from Pakatan Harapan. Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said the arrangement was intended to maximise the opposition's chances of defeating PH, which currently governs the state. Amar said the arrangement in Negri Sembilan, as well as in the recent Johor election, was an electoral understanding rather than a formal political alliance.

items.title: Negri Sembilan election: PH banking on state government's track record
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Pakatan Harapan will focus on highlighting its performance record in the Negri Sembilan election rather than getting involved in the internal affairs of other political parties. PKR Youth deputy chief Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari said PH's focus now was to ensure voters assessed the performance of the Negri Sembilan government rather than being drawn into the political manoeuvring of other parties. The people of Negri Sembilan are capable of judging who is the best to lead the state government after the upcoming state election," he said.

items.title: Most Bangkok bar fire victims died from smoke inhalation, autopsies find
items.summary: BANGKOK: The majority of people killed in Bangkok's devastating bar fire died from smoke inhalation, forensic police told AFP on Friday after completing autopsies of all 33 victims. The blaze swept through the popular Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao restaurant and bar in the Thai capital on Sunday night, quickly engulfing the venue. The remaining six victims died later in hospital from severe burn injuries, Wiroon said.

items.title: Govt starts drafting legal framework for road accident compensation, Bill expected in November
items.summary: He said the proposed amendment would introduce, for the first time, a legal mechanism allowing compensation to be awarded during criminal proceedings, while also strengthening the Road Safety Demerit Points System (Kejara). Loke said the proposed mechanism would cover offences involving dangerous or reckless driving, including cases involving drivers under the influence of alcohol or drugs. Loke stressed that the proposed compensation mechanism would not affect victims' existing rights to pursue insurance claims or civil lawsuits.

items.title: Negri Sembilan election: 464 nomination forms sold ahead of nomination day
items.summary: SEREMBAN: A total of 464 nomination forms for the Negri Sembilan election have been sold so far, said Election Commission (EC) chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun. Ramlan said preparations for the nomination process involving the 36 state constituencies in Negri Sembilan were proceeding smoothly and had been completed ahead of nomination day tomorrow. Ramlan said 761 EC officers would be deployed tomorrow to oversee the nomination process at eight centres located in eight parliamentary constituencies.

items.title: DOSM: Insurance costs surge 5.7pc but transport slowdown tempers Malaysia’s June inflation at 1.9pc
items.summary: The department said the personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services as well as education groups also recorded slower increases of 3.4 per cent (May 2026: 4.8 per cent) and 2.1 per cent (May 2026: 2.2 per cent), respectively. DOSM said the information and communication group recorded an increase of 2.4 per cent in June 2026 (May 2026: 2.1 per cent); the housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels group rose to 1.4 per cent from 1.2 per cent in May 2026. DOSM pointed out that seven states recorded increases above the national inflation level (1.9 per cent), namely Pahang (2.6 per cent), Johor (2.5 per cent), Negeri Sembilan (2.5 per cent), Wilayah Persekutuan Labuan (2.3 per cent), Wilayah Persekutuan Kuala Lumpur (2.2 per cent), Kedah (2.0 per cent) and Wilayah Persekutuan Putrajaya (2.0 per cent).

items.title: UMT turtle conservation efforts see fourfold nesting increase over 30 years
items.summary: KUALA NERUS: Green turtle conservation efforts by Universiti Malaysia Terengganu (UMT) at Pantai Chagar Hutang on Redang Island have yielded a three- to fourfold nesting increase over 30 years. UMT Marine Conservation Centre head Associate Professor Dr Mohd Uzair Rusli said green turtle nests rose from 400 to 500 annually in the early 1990s to between 1,500 and 2,000 nests a year since 2016. Uzair said UMT research suggests the change is linked to the condition of marine feeding habitats, as turtles migrate thousands of kilometres to foraging grounds before returning to nesting beaches years later.

items.title: Fact check: Trump rehashes false 2020 election fraud allegations
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump resurrected long-debunked claims about fraud and foreign interference in the 2020 election, alleging in an address Thursday that China stole millions of voter files and suggesting Venezuela could manipulate American voting machines. Rick Hasen, an election law expert at the University of California, Los Angeles, told AFP that Trump's speech consisted mostly of "recycled and debunked claims." Here is a fact-check of some of Trump's claims: - DATA COMPROMISED BY CHINA - Trump accused China of "the largest compromise of election data in history, resulting in China's illicit acquisition of 220 million US voter files."

items.title: Pickup truck driver claims trial to dangerous driving over fatal Bentong crash
items.summary: RAUB: A pickup truck driver claimed trial at the magistrate's court here today to charges of causing the death of a motorcyclist and seriously injuring the victim's six-year-old son through dangerous driving in Mempaga, Bentong, on July 14. Hairizal Ahmad, 44, pleaded not guilty after the two charges were read separately before Magistrate Siti Aisyah Ahmad. For the second charge, Hairizal was accused of driving recklessly in a manner that endangered Zaiful Arimey's six-year-old son, Putera Nik Zarif Aqil, who was riding pillion.

items.title: 19-year-old Rohingya woman charged with murder over newborn's death
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: A 19-year-old Rohingya woman was charged with murder at the Johor Baru magistrate's court today for allegedly throwing her newborn baby out of a hotel window. District police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said the woman allegedly threw the newborn from a second-floor hotel room window into an alley in Bukit Indah. He said police received information about the discovery of a plastic-wrapped newborn in an alley at Taman Nusa Idaman, Bukit Indah, at 2.39am on July 5.

items.title: Missing Kota Tinggi teen found dead after nearly week-long search
items.summary: The victim, identified as Muhammad Nasrulhaq Kamaruzaman, 15, was found after nearly a week of search efforts by his family and villagers. The video call at 4.26am on Sunday was the last time he could be contacted," she said. He was a quiet person," she said.

items.title: Negri Sembilan election: Berjasa to contest Nilai seat after PN snub
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Parti Barisan Jemaah Islamiah Se-Malaysia (Berjasa) will contest the Nilai seat in the upcoming Negri Sembilan election. Zamani said Nilai was chosen as it is a suitable starting point to field a Malay candidate in a Malay-majority area. However, following last night's candidate announcement, no candidate from Berjasa was named.

items.title: Fake cake link costs woman RM1.39 million
items.summary: MIRI: A woman lost RM1.39 million after falling victim to a scam syndicate that used a fake cake-ordering link to gain access to her company's bank accounts. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said police received a report yesterday from the 43-year-old victim. Farhan said initial investigations found that 19 transactions were made to 15 different bank accounts on July 14, involving a total of RM1,389,132.

items.title: Another fall feared at Papar school as Form Two girl reportedly rushed to hospital
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — Confirmation on reports that another female student fell and had to be rushed to hospital during school hours at SMK Tun Datu Mustapha in Kinarut, Papar, on Wednesday, received no response from the State Education Director at press time. Daily Express reliably learnt that the Form Two student, whose mother is a teacher at the school, was taken to hospital for treatment. — Daily Express

items.title: PBAPP: New water tariffs essential to avert future shortages
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang's revised water tariffs are necessary to fund urgent water supply projects and prevent future shortages as demand continues to grow, Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) said. PBAPP chief executive officer Datuk K. Pathmanathan said additional revenue from the revised tariffs, which took effect on July 1, would be channelled towards critical projects under the Water Contingency Plan 2030 (WCP 2030). Business consumers using 500m³ of water a month would pay an additional RM77.70, or RM2.59 a day.

items.title: Langkawi daycare centre under probe for allegedly starving, locking children in toilet
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are investigating child abuse allegations at a childcare centre in Kuah, Langkawi, following reports that children were deprived of food and locked in a toilet. The allegations gained traction after a Threads post today by user @_misaharoff, who claimed her sister's child was traumatised after being locked in a toilet as punishment. "They allegedly locked my nephew in the toilet and attempted to torture him.

items.title: Negri Sembilan election: Fair weather expected on nomination day, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Most parts of Negri Sembilan are expected to enjoy fine weather tomorrow morning for the state's 16th election nomination day. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said fair weather is expected in the morning in Jelebu, Jempol, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin, while Port Dickson and Seremban are expected to experience rain. "Minimum temperatures across the state are expected to range between 23 and 24 degrees Celsius, while maximum temperatures are forecast at 32 to 33 degrees Celsius," he said.

items.title: Japan keeps male-only throne despite public support for woman emperor
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's parliament enacted changes to the imperial succession law on Friday but maintained the bar on a woman becoming emperor despite surveys suggesting wide public support for the idea. The future of the imperial household – mythically descended from the Shinto sun goddess Amaterasu – hinges currently on Prince Hisahito, the 19-year-old nephew of serving Emperor Naruhito, 66. This rules out popular Princess Aiko, 24, daughter of Naruhito, or Hisahito's two elder sisters, ever becoming emperor.

items.title: At least 14 dead in Peru road accident
items.summary: LIMA: At least 14 people died and five others were injured on Thursday when a van plunged off a roadway in northern Peru, authorities told AFP. Police and fire crews arrived at the scene of the accident, outside San Juan, to recover the victims' bodies, including those of the driver and a child, Chilon said. The vehicle was carrying 19 people from the San Juan district to Ciudad de Dios.

items.title: MetMalaysia forecasts fine morning weather for Negeri Sembilan nomination, rain in Seremban, Port Dickson
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 17 — Most parts of Negeri Sembilan are expected to enjoy fine weather on the morning of tomorrow’s nomination day for the state’s 16th election. Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Dr Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip said fair weather is expected in the morning in Jelebu, Jempol, Kuala Pilah, Rembau and Tampin, while Port Dickson and Seremban are expected to experience rain. Later today, Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) is expected to announce the list of its candidates for the state election.

items.title: Sectors across the board spurs Malaysia's 5.8pct economic growth in Q2 2026: DOSM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy expanded by 5.8 per cent in the second quarter of 2026 (Q2 2026), following the 5.4 per cent growth in the preceding quarter, according to the Statistics Department. "Overall, Malaysia's economy grew at 5.6 per cent for the first half of the year (2H 2026), compared to 4.5 per cent in 2H 2025," it said in a statement today. "The construction sector grew moderately at 6.6 per cent (Q1 2026: 7.7 per cent), supported by the non-residential buildings sub-sector, particularly data centre construction projects and specialised construction activities," it said.

items.title: Transport Ministry: Cabinet approves plan to make road crash offenders compensate victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — The Cabinet has agreed in principle to introduce court-ordered compensation for road accident victims under proposed amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987. In a statement today, the Transport Ministry said the Cabinet approved the proposal on July 15 as part of wider reforms that would make offenders accountable not only through imprisonment, fines or driving disqualification, but also for the impact of their actions on victims and their families. The amendments would also strengthen the Road Traffic Offence Demerit Points System (Kejara) to better identify high-risk drivers and repeat offenders.

items.title: Student chasing Telegram ‘love’ loses RM56,375 after making 45 payments for promised relationship
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 17 — A public higher education institution student lost RM56,375 after falling victim to a love scam two months ago. Kuala Terengganu district police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said the 22-year-old victim got acquainted with a woman through the Telegram application on May 11 but did not have her phone number, only knowing the name displayed on the account. Azli said the man, who was deceived by the suspect’s persuasion, made 45 transactions from his bank account to an account provided by the suspect between May 11 and July 4.

items.title: Marine Police join MMEA as 190 inmates moved from Miri to Limbang in tense 3.15am sea convoy
items.summary: MIRI, July 17 — A total of 190 inmates from Miri Central Prison were transferred to Limbang Central Prison by sea on Thursday as part of an initiative by the Malaysian Prison Department to ease overcrowding. According to Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) Miri Zone director Captain Mohd Khairol Anuar Saad, the transfer convoy under Ops Jerjak Samudera 2.0 was led by Deputy Director of Prison Security and Order at the Malaysian Prison Headquarters, Deputy Commissioner of Prisons Mohd Nasir Yusof, with assistance from MMEA Miri. Mohd Khairol said MMEA deployed several assets in support of the operation, namely KM Tegas, Kilat 41 and Kilat 42 from IPMZ Miri, as well as Petir 10 from IPMZ Bintulu.

items.title: Around 4,000 AirBorneo passengers affected as airline revises flight frequencies until October
items.summary: KUCHING, July 17 — Around 4,000 AirBorneo passengers are expected to be affected by flight schedule adjustments following a temporary revision to selected frequencies across the airline’s ATR network to improve fleet reliability and operational performance. The state-owned airline said the temporary changes, which took effect on July 6 and will remain until Oct 24, involve only selected routes and are aimed at reducing last-minute cancellations while allowing essential aircraft maintenance works to be carried out. AirBorneo said the adjustments were necessary following the temporary grounding of several ATR72 and DHC-6 Twin Otter aircraft due to unscheduled maintenance requirements, compounded by global supply chain constraints affecting the availability of critical aircraft components and spare parts.

items.title: Finance Ministry says National Trust Fund Bill safeguards RM22.43b reserves, ensures growth
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 17 — The National Trust Fund Bill 2026, which was passed by the Dewan Rakyat yesterday, will strengthen the National Trust Fund (KWAN) as the country’s intergenerational savings fund to ensure sustainable financial reserves for both present and future generations. The bill also marks the first comprehensive reform of KWAN since its establishment in 1988. In a statement today, the Finance Ministry (MOF) said the reforms introduced under the bill reflect the Madani government’s commitment to strengthening public financial management, safeguarding the nation’s wealth sustainably, and ensuring that today’s national revenues continue to benefit future generations.

items.title: Race for 36 Negeri Sembilan seats begins tomorrow as nomination day kicks off campaign
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 17 — The race for the 36 seats in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election officially begins tomorrow with nomination day, marking the start of a 14-day campaign period. Attention will be focused on the eight nomination centres across the state, where prospective candidates will submit their nomination papers between 9 am and 10 am before the returning officers announce the final list of candidates eligible to contest. Pakatan Harapan (PH) will contest all 36 seats, fielding 16 candidates from PKR, 11 from DAP and nine from Amanah.

items.title: Coroner’s Court marks first anniversary of Zara Qairina’s death with minute of silence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — The courtroom in Kota Kinabalu fell silent today as lawyers, witnesses and family members stood to mark the first anniversary of the death of 13-year-old Zara Qairina Mahathir, New Straits Times (NST) reported. On the first anniversary of Zara Qairina’s death, Amir directed everyone in the courtroom to stand in her memory before proceedings continued on the 83rd day of the inquest. “Today, July 17, 2026, marks exactly one year since the demise of Zara Qairina Mahathir.

items.title: Driver survives but attendant dies as lorry carrying spare parts plunges 40 metres into Kapit ravine
items.summary: KAPIT, July 17 — A 28-year-old lorry attendant was killed after a 10-tonne lorry carrying vehicle spare parts skidded off the road and plunged about 40 metres into a ravine near Jambatan Besi, Sungai Majau in Mujong, Baleh near here. Kapit Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) station chief Timoty Barat said the department received a request for assistance from police yesterday at 12.31pm after the victim was reported to have fallen into the ravine following the crash. “Our Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) team travelled to the Kapit police station before proceeding to the scene in a police four-wheel-drive vehicle due to the rough terrain,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Rohingya teen charged with murder after newborn allegedly thrown from Johor hotel window
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — A 19-year-old Rohingya woman has been charged with murder after police alleged she gave birth in a Johor hotel bathroom before throwing her newborn from a second-floor window. The baby’s body was discovered inside a black plastic bag near Taman Nusa Idaman in Johor Bahru in the early hours of July 5, triggering an investigation that led officers to the teenager. Iskandar Puteri police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Kumarasan said investigators found the baby was the result of the suspect’s relationship with her former boyfriend.

items.title: Muhyiddin: Perikatan has shut Bersatu out, Umno alliance ‘not genuine Malay unity’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin said today that Perikatan Nasional (PN) had sidelined one of its founding parties. He said the PAS-led coalition’s pursuit of an alliance with Umno reflects political expediency rather than genuine Malay unity. Muhyiddin rejected the allegation, insisting Bersatu had never chosen to leave the coalition.

items.title: Sabah Education Department probes viral student brawl outside school, pledges full police cooperation
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 17 — The Sabah Education Department has expressed serious concern over a brawl involving several students outside school premises after a video of the incident went viral on social media recently. In a statement, the department said the school involved, together with the District Education Office, acted swiftly to identify the students involved and immediately launched an internal investigation. “Sabah Education Department views any form of misconduct involving students seriously, even if the incident occurred outside school grounds,” it said.

items.title: ‘Campaign of hope’ over ‘bogeyman’ politics, says Loke as Pakatan targets simple majority in Negeri Sembilan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is aiming to win 20 of Negeri Sembilan’s 36 seats to retain the state government, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said. “I want to run … a campaign of hope that things will be better,” he said. Loke said he would personally oversee the coalition’s campaign messaging.

items.title: Loke rules out DAP exit from Pakatan, says slower reforms are the price of governing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — DAP will not leave Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s Pakatan Harapan (PH) coalition before the current government’s term ends despite recent electoral setbacks, secretary-general Anthony Loke has said. “In the context of Malaysian politics, I do not think the DAP can go far if we just go solo,” Loke said. Loke said Malaysia’s demographic makeup and political system meant contesting alone would leave DAP confined to the Opposition benches rather than participating in government.

items.title: Finance Ministry says Binance not licensed in Malaysia, website and app access still blocked
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 17 — Cryptocurrency and blockchain giant Binance has not been licensed or registered with the Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) to carry out capital market activities in Malaysia, said the Ministry of Finance (MOF). The MOF said Binance’s website and application remain blocked for users in Malaysia. MOF said the SC has practised an open policy in encouraging the participation of all capital market participants, including innovative players, in the development of the Malaysian capital market ecosystem.
